purpose: Test if EG paradigm transfers from MNIST to harder color images

results.name: EG-MLP (flatten CIFAR to 3072-dim vector)
results.failure_reason: No spatial inductive bias. Flattening 32x32x3 images destroys spatial structure. Also 3072-dim input makes EG layers huge and slow.
results.lesson: MLP on CIFAR-10 is fundamentally limited. Need convolutional architecture.

results.name: EG-Conv V1 (Sigmoid activation in conv layers)
results.architecture: EGConv(3→64→128→256, sigmoid) + Pool + EGDense(4096→500) + MLP probe
results.failure_reason: Sigmoid activation in conv layers causes saturation. All neurons output ~0.5, entropy is maximal everywhere, plasticity is uniform. No differentiation between neurons → no learning.
results.lesson: CRITICAL: Sigmoid works for dense MNIST but KILLS conv layers. Sigmoid squashes spatial feature maps to uniform values. Must use ReLU for conv.

results.name: EG-Conv V2 (ReLU + Local Batch Normalization)
results.architecture: EGConv(3→64→128→256, ReLU+LocalBN) + Pool + EGDense(4096→500, ReLU) + MLP probe
results.trajectory: Fast rise to 48% by ep 6, then plateau/slow decline to ~45%
results.verdict: Limited — significant learning but huge gap to backprop (85.83%)
results.failure_reason: Channel-wise average Hebbian (average over spatial dims, then correlate) loses spatial information. The conv filters learn rough feature detectors but can't capture fine spatial patterns.
results.lesson: Need PATCH-LEVEL Hebbian: correlate each output position with its specific receptive field, not channel means.
improvements_suggested: Patch-level Hebbian: unfold input, correlate with output per position
Multi-scale features: skip connections between EG conv layers
Deeper with layer-wise pretraining: freeze layer 1, train layer 2, etc.

gap_analysis.reason: Conv layers need spatial correlations that channel-average Hebbian can't provide

decision: CIFAR-10 remains open challenge. MNIST is solved. Next: better spatial Hebbian for conv.
